_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to review and edit our own texts and others' by removing repetition, refining ideas, reordering sentences and adding or substituting words for impact.

_source.w: We are learning to review and edit our own texts and others' by removing repetition, refining ideas, reordering sentences and adding or substituting words for impact.
y: Skilled editing transforms competent writing into excellent writing.
Being able to identify and fix specific issues — repetition, unclear ideas, weak word choices — shows advanced writing control.
Editing others' work develops our critical eye and builds our own writing awareness.
eg: We identify and remove unnecessary repetition in our writing.
We refine vague ideas by adding specific details or evidence.
We reorder sentences within paragraphs to improve logical flow.
We substitute weak or imprecise words with stronger, more effective alternatives.
We peer-edit a classmate's text using specific criteria and provide constructive feedback.
We reflect on our editing process and discuss the impact of our changes.
